title: Capsella subalpina
taxonomy: Capsella subalpina is a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It is classified as a part of the Brassicaceae family, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as mustards or cabbages. The genus Capsella includes a few species of flowering plants, with Capsella subalpina being one of them. This plant is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. Capsella subalpina is often referred to as a wild cabbage or a weed that can thrive in a variety of habitats.

intro: Capsella subalpina, commonly known as small cress or alpine peppercress, is a flowering plant in the Brassicaceae family. Traditionally, Capsella subalpina has been used for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing pain and inflammation, as well as supporting the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Capsella subalpina include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Capsella subalpina is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to take advantage of its potential health benefits.
